subject: Wire three-tier memory system end-to-end
description: The memory architecture is designed but not wired. Six critical gaps: 1) SCHEMA MIGRATION on existing memory.db to add companion_id + scope columns. 2) REALM DB CREATION at ~/.sigil/realm.db. 3) VECTOR EMBEDDINGS wired in store() using text-embedding-3-small. 4) HYBRID RETRIEVAL merging FTS5 + vector cosine with MMR. 5) AGENT MEMORY INTEGRATION - add memory to Agent struct. 6) COMPANION SCOPING - three-tier scope enforcement. This is foundational for the companion system.

subject: Build autonomous reflection system — Pulse-driven self-reflection that reads identity files, diffs against memory, rewrites changed sections. ReflectionQuest type in spirit executor. Budget-capped at 2k tokens per cycle, no sub-agents. Configurable interval (default: 4h). Updates MEMORY.md, HEARTBEAT.md, IDENTITY.md when drift detected.

Build session alarm and progress heartbeat system — SessionTracker in summoner with deadline + checkin_interval. tokio::time::interval in dedicated spawn (not inline main loop). Fires Channel::send() to Telegram on threshold. Supports: periodic check-ins during long sprints, alarm-style 'get back to Architect' reminders, idle detection when queue empties. Anti-flood: minimum 30min between messages, smart batching.

Build autonomous quest generation — when a spirit completes all work in a domain and the queue empties, evaluate remaining gaps from MEMORY.md and domain AGENTS.md, propose or self-assign the next highest-impact task. Requires: gap analysis prompt, confidence threshold (>70% to auto-assign, otherwise surface to Shadow for routing), backlog evaluation, and integration with the new reflection system.
